title_original: Lausanne-based Rhonexum raises â¬867.5k to advance cryogenic electronics for scalable quantum computing | EU-Startups
summary_original: Rhonexum, a Lausanne-based spin-out from the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne’s AQUA Lab, has raised €867.5k ($1M) in pre-seed funding led by QDNL Participations and Venture Kick, with additional support from Swiss innovation programs such as EPFL Startup Launchpad, Fondation pour l’Innovation Technologique, and the Swiss National Science Foundation. Founded in November 2025 by Vicente Carbon and Dr Hung-Chi Han, the company develops cryogenic electronics designed to operate close to absolute zero (-270°C), directly addressing the scalability bottleneck in quantum computing. Rhonexum’s technology aims to optimize thermal management and reduce cabling complexity, with its first industrial-grade product scheduled for delivery to early customers later in the year.
